text: Ask dpkg what state it's in: `sudo dpkg --audit`. It lists packages 'unpacked but not yet configured' — here, `tree`.
This is the classic interrupted-dpkg state. The message even tells you the fix: run `dpkg --configure -a`, which finishes configuring every package left half-done.
Run `sudo dpkg --configure -a`. Then confirm it's clean: `sudo dpkg --audit` (no output) and `dpkg-query -W -f='${Status}\n' tree` shows `install ok installed`.
